_comment: Canonical solver-binary manifest. Consumed by dexbtx_miner.solver_updater on every miner startup. To add a new platform: add an entry under `platforms`. To force-upgrade across a fork boundary: set `min_required_sha256` on the relevant platform entry. v1 single-binary schema (top-level sha256+url) is still recognized by the updater as legacy (treated as x86_64-linux); the v2 platforms-dict schema below is preferred and supports any platform key the wrapper can detect.
notes: Multi-platform release. x86_64-linux is LTO-enabled v0.32.2 rebuild (restores share_target_override, V2 nonce-seed at h=125000). aarch64 builds via GitHub Actions on Linux arm64 runner (cuda 12.8 or 13.0). darwin-arm64 is the Mac Metal test build.

x86_64-linux.notes: GCC 11.4, CUDA 12.8, archs: sm_61;sm_75;sm_86;sm_89;sm_90;sm_120. LTO enabled.

aarch64-linux.notes: Default aarch64-linux entry. CUDA 13.0, archs sm_80;sm_90;sm_120;sm_121. Covers GB10/Spark + A100/H100/RTX-Blackwell. Requires CUDA 13.0+ runtime. For operators with only CUDA 12 runtime, set DEXBTX_PLATFORM_KEY=aarch64-linux-cuda12 to fetch the cuda12 build instead.

aarch64-linux-cuda12.notes: Opt-in via DEXBTX_PLATFORM_KEY=aarch64-linux-cuda12. CUDA 12.8 build, archs sm_80;sm_90;sm_120. No sm_121 (use cuda13 build for GB10).

arm64-darwin.notes: Apple Silicon (M-series). Mac Metal backend. EXPERIMENTAL — not yet end-to-end-validated on real Mac mining; treat as test build. Operators should verify --backend metal engages with `powermetrics` or `asitop` before relying on it.
